mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. Lower FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window directly compresses cost of capital, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premium downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.42% as of April 2019 reflects post-2018 rate cut cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate earnings through increased consumption and pricing power. Accelerating payroll growth over 2 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,095k as of April 2019 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline signals weakening consumer demand and future earnings compression. When RSAFS falls below its 6-month moving average, equity valuations contract as investors reduce forward earnings estimates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below 6-month moving average by >2%
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. When INDPRO accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, it signals supply-side expansion that supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate simultaneously (each >2% year-over-year), demand-driven earnings growth accelerates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures the multiplier effect of coordinated demand expansion.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both grow >2% year-over-year in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,139k and RSAFS at 513,356M as of March-April 2019
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL rises >0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%, real rates compress, reducing equity discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if nominal rate hikes offset inflation gains.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 254.96 and FEDFUNDS at 2.42% as of April 2019
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of co-triggered instances, or real rate compression fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.015 over 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and future earnings pressure from wage inflation and reduced consumer spending. When UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, equity valuations compress as investors reduce forward earnings estimates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of April 2019, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when UNRATE > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture demand momentum and forward growth expectations. When GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5% and HOUST accelerates >5% year-over-year, the economy enters a high-growth regime that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction reflects the reinforcing cycle of growth and investment.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth >2.5% AND HOUST year-over-year growth >5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,912.3B and HOUST at 1,139k as of Q1 2019 and March 2019
regime_hint: synchronized growth expansion with investment acceleration
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 54% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of growth-investment interaction < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.
